text:
The saying of the man who ascended and descended
In the circuit of song, though his star's in decline,
Whose words flame zig-zag through his book
As the flame of fire flashes through hail.
Backwards he turned the shadow on the dial
With his light, when the day was almost gone.
And fifty compositions he composed
Of diverse theme in poetry and prose.
All scattered bits of rhetoric he gathered,
Not one of them escaped or slipped him by.
Of words he made a chequer pattern woven
Of intellect, not purple or of lace.
Poetry became his faithful servant
Though rebel against all but him.
At home his power and glory reached to peaks,
But wandering humbled him and brought him low.
His star dropped to the depths, though once
On heights with might he ruled with God and men.
Necklace of pearls for poetry was he,
Broken and scattered now by wear of wandering.
Garden of God, so was his native land —
Now he is exiled and banished from its fields,
For he forsook the land of his sojourn
With trembling haste to stand on holy soil.
Judah, the son of Solomon, his name be known;
His birthplace and his native land is Spain.

I compose precious things,
Fables and riddles;
And flocks of testimonies
I bring back to the barn.
I lift up song,
I set it as king.
I cover its nakedness
With silk and satin.
My tongue weaves
The thread of my thoughts
And the garments of my Fate
I embroider with my poetry.
And the dispersed rhetoric
I bring together in a heap;
From them Judah assembles
The scattered ones.

Woe to the fools, like wild asses they bray;
Beside fountains of Eden, yet thirst they all day.
Manna is before their eyes - but their eyes are blind.
They go forth to gather but none do they find.

The heart guides to the voice that cries:
What man is he that desires life?
Let him run to the master who conquers by might,
He will make him know the path of life.
Every heart that yearns — that thirsts for understanding,
To him he gives waters of life.
Under his eminence his enemy is subdued.
Yea, all that find him find life.
All that see him, love him, for
Out of him are the issues of life,
For in his tongue, and in his thoughts
Are the pangs of death and the balsam of life.
He is as flaming sword which whirls about
To keep the way of the tree of life.

Prince of the Mighty God, rod of strength and the
staff of majesty, he wields power by the strength of
the Almighty.
Josiah, prince of God, garland for the head of excel-
lence, the Rock sent him as a light in the darkness
of captivity --
May the Eternal God in his days bring near the
Prince of Peace and may He increase peace to him
whose government is increased, as it is written:
"That the government may be increased."
Josiah, prince of God, adornment of Wisdom, the
Rock sent him as preserver of life and repairer of
every breach.

Adornment of the people of righteousness, as a rose
amidst a thornbush, elect of the descendants of Perez,
he strengthens the breach!
Kings and their cohorts bow down before him when
the breaker passes before them and he goes up, as it
is written: "when the breaker is gone up..."
May there be length of glorious days for the prince
of uprightness and of virtue. God placed him as a
necklace about the royal neck.
And God, who chose him, exalted his office and
made his rank to be forever established as the moon.
And God decreed and announced that His throne
is fixed and because of this the Lord swore unto
David, as it is written: "The Lord swore unto
David... of the fruit of your body will I set upon
your throne."
You chosen ones of God's people, faithful Israel,
give praise to God and sing to my Lord,
Who revived excellence through government of Josiah,
the adornment of sound knowledge and prince to
the Lord.
The poor that were languishing blossomed in his days
so that afterwards they returned and sought the Lord,
as it is written: "They shall return and seek..."
May exalted blessings, and myriad of good, standing
like a heap, come upon the head of the costly corner-
stone!
And the government is a wreath upon the head of
Josiah. Is he not a fruitful branch in a well of sound
knowledge?
The youngest of brothers rules them, most illustrious
in the row of carnelian whose ancestors from of old
were in Bethlehem-Ephros, as it is written: "And thou,
Bethlehem..."

Send your gift to Josiah from the clusters of your
intellect and from the best of their fruit.
All praises are fitting to him, as all the rivers flow into
the sea.
A prince to whom all the exalted nobles bow — they
place the thong of his sandal upon their cheek.
His enviers utterly perish by the breath of his mouth
and the onlookers say: "Where are they?"
In his name the children of exile exult and for the
majesty of my lord they shout from the west.
Their heart's ill is healed and their pride is lifted up —
yea, he is the balsam for their illness and the flower
of their adornment.
Verily, greatness builds a castle for his name estab-
lished upon the stars of the Dragon.
The daughters of song weave robes of his praise from
the chequered work of their ornaments and the best of
their silk.
Until he made a proclamation pass through their
camp that the work was sufficient.

They are filleted with words sweet as honey, and to
their pursuers the near are far.
When they run in tournament, the heart of the lion
gets feeble, lightnings grow weary to reach their dust.
And were lightnings to pursue them, they would leave
them behind, bound in chains.
Compare them to cherubs, hewn out of flames that
run and return like appearance of lightning.
Lofty stars, ornaments in their ears and flames of
the luminaries seized in their hands.
They make mighty ones slain ghosts and before
them giants become grasshoppers.
And they are to simpletons heavy, hateful; but to
the understanding lovely, delightful.
They are embraced by the arms of friendship and are
kissed by the lips of hearts.
My rivals strive to reach them but their fountain is
too far, its waters too deep.
Sweet and tender, clear and pure, stronger than the
rock and smoother than the honey-comb.
And their lines are paved with bars of silver, refined
in the furnace of mind and cast in the heart.
And how the jealous ones weary for my speech,
seeing all of them are as the earth and my words
are skies.

He who hearkens to the counsel of a friend, pure of
heart.
Can unsheathe it as a sword against his foes;
And like gold he will find his word to be.
When he tests it in the furnace of his heart.
